intro: Alcea kurdica, also known as Kurdish mallow, is a perennial plant species native to the Middle East and Mediterranean regions. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Alcea kurdica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Alcea kurdica is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it may also be used in the form of a topical paste or ointment for skin-related issues.
